Advanced String Algorithms

Overview

Advanced String Algorithms encompass sophisticated techniques for string processing beyond basic operations. These algorithms provide optimal solutions for pattern matching, palindrome detection, and complex string manipulations with theoretical guarantees.

Key Properties

  • Time Complexity: Often O(n) or O(n + m) for optimal algorithms
  • Space Complexity: O(n) for preprocessing structures
  • Core Idea: Preprocess strings to enable fast queries and pattern matching
  • When to Use: Complex string patterns, multiple queries, optimization needed
  • Key Algorithms: KMP, Manacher’s, Z-Algorithm, Rolling Hash, Suffix Arrays

Core Characteristics

  • Preprocessing: Build auxiliary structures for fast operations
  • Pattern Recognition: Identify repeating structures and patterns
  • Linear Time: Achieve optimal time complexity through clever techniques
  • Multiple Queries: Efficient for repeated operations on same string
  • Theoretical Foundation: Based on deep string theory and automata

Problem Categories

Category 1: Pattern Matching

  • Description: Find occurrences of pattern in text efficiently
  • Examples: LC 28 (Find Index of First Occurrence), LC 459 (Repeated Substring Pattern)
  • Pattern: KMP, Z-Algorithm, Rolling Hash for O(n + m) solutions

Category 2: Palindrome Problems

  • Description: Find all palindromes or longest palindromic substrings
  • Examples: LC 5 (Longest Palindromic Substring), LC 647 (Palindromic Substrings)
  • Pattern: Manacher’s Algorithm for O(n) palindrome detection

Category 3: String Periodicity

  • Description: Detect repeating patterns and string periods
  • Examples: LC 459 (Repeated Substring Pattern), LC 1316 (Distinct Echo Substrings)
  • Pattern: Period detection using failure function or Z-array

Category 4: Suffix-Based Problems

  • Description: Problems involving string suffixes and lexicographic ordering
  • Examples: LC 1044 (Longest Duplicate Substring), LC 1316 (Distinct Echo Substrings)
  • Pattern: Suffix arrays, longest common prefix, rolling hash

Templates & Algorithms

Template Comparison Table

Algorithm Use Case Time Complexity Space Complexity When to Use
KMP Pattern matching O(n + m) O(m) Single pattern search
Manacher’s All palindromes O(n) O(n) Palindrome problems
Z-Algorithm String matching O(n) O(n) Pattern matching variants
Rolling Hash Substring comparison O(n) O(1) Multiple pattern search

Template 1: KMP (Knuth-Morris-Pratt) Algorithm

1def kmp_search(text, pattern):
2    """KMP algorithm for pattern matching"""
3
4    def compute_failure_function(pattern):
5        """Compute failure function (partial match table)"""
6        m = len(pattern)
7        failure = [0] * m
8        j = 0
9
10        for i in range(1, m):
11            while j > 0 and pattern[i] != pattern[j]:
12                j = failure[j - 1]
13
14            if pattern[i] == pattern[j]:
15                j += 1
16
17            failure[i] = j
18
19        return failure
20
21    if not pattern:
22        return 0
23
24    n, m = len(text), len(pattern)
25    failure = compute_failure_function(pattern)
26    matches = []
27
28    j = 0  # Index for pattern
29    for i in range(n):  # Index for text
30        while j > 0 and text[i] != pattern[j]:
31            j = failure[j - 1]
32
33        if text[i] == pattern[j]:
34            j += 1
35
36        if j == m:
37            matches.append(i - m + 1)
38            j = failure[j - 1]
39
40    return matches
41
42def kmp_pattern_matching_template():
43    """Template for various KMP applications"""
44
45    def find_first_occurrence(text, pattern):
46        """Find first occurrence of pattern in text"""
47        matches = kmp_search(text, pattern)
48        return matches[0] if matches else -1
49
50    def count_occurrences(text, pattern):
51        """Count all occurrences of pattern"""
52        return len(kmp_search(text, pattern))
53
54    def is_substring_pattern(text, pattern):
55        """Check if pattern exists in text"""
56        return len(kmp_search(text, pattern)) > 0
57
58    def repeated_string_pattern(s):
59        """Check if string is made of repeated pattern (LC 459)"""
60        n = len(s)
61        failure = compute_failure_function(s + s)
62
63        # Check if s is a rotation of itself in s+s
64        return failure[-1] != 0 and n % (n - failure[-1]) == 0
65
66    return {
67        'find_first': find_first_occurrence,
68        'count': count_occurrences,
69        'exists': is_substring_pattern,
70        'repeated': repeated_string_pattern
71    }

Template 2: Manacher’s Algorithm

1def manacher_algorithm(s):
2    """Manacher's algorithm to find all palindromes in O(n)"""
3
4    def preprocess(s):
5        """Preprocess string to handle even-length palindromes"""
6        # Transform "abba" -> "^#a#b#b#a#$"
7        result = "^"
8        for c in s:
9            result += "#" + c
10        result += "#$"
11        return result
12
13    processed = preprocess(s)
14    n = len(processed)
15    p = [0] * n  # p[i] = radius of palindrome centered at i
16    center = right = 0
17
18    for i in range(1, n - 1):
19        # Mirror of i with respect to center
20        mirror = 2 * center - i
21
22        # If i is within the right boundary, use previously computed values
23        if i < right:
24            p[i] = min(right - i, p[mirror])
25
26        # Try to expand palindrome centered at i
27        while processed[i + p[i] + 1] == processed[i - p[i] - 1]:
28            p[i] += 1
29
30        # If palindrome centered at i extends past right, adjust center and right
31        if i + p[i] > right:
32            center, right = i, i + p[i]
33
34    return p, processed
35
36def manacher_applications():
37    """Various applications of Manacher's algorithm"""
38
39    def longest_palindromic_substring(s):
40        """Find longest palindromic substring (LC 5)"""
41        if not s:
42            return ""
43
44        p, processed = manacher_algorithm(s)
45        max_len = 0
46        center_index = 0
47
48        for i in range(1, len(p) - 1):
49            if p[i] > max_len:
50                max_len = p[i]
51                center_index = i
52
53        # Convert back to original string coordinates
54        start = (center_index - max_len) // 2
55        return s[start:start + max_len]
56
57    def count_palindromic_substrings(s):
58        """Count all palindromic substrings (LC 647)"""
59        if not s:
60            return 0
61
62        p, processed = manacher_algorithm(s)
63        count = 0
64
65        for i in range(1, len(p) - 1):
66            # Each palindrome of radius r contributes (r+1)/2 palindromes
67            count += (p[i] + 1) // 2
68
69        return count
70
71    def is_palindrome_range(s, left, right):
72        """Check if substring s[left:right+1] is palindrome"""
73        p, processed = manacher_algorithm(s)
74
75        # Convert to processed string coordinates
76        center = left + right + 2
77        radius = right - left
78
79        return p[center] >= radius
80
81    def all_palindromic_substrings(s):
82        """Get all palindromic substrings with their positions"""
83        p, processed = manacher_algorithm(s)
84        palindromes = []
85
86        for i in range(1, len(p) - 1):
87            for r in range(p[i] + 1):
88                # Convert back to original coordinates
89                start = (i - r - 1) // 2
90                end = (i + r - 1) // 2
91                if start <= end:
92                    palindromes.append((start, end, s[start:end + 1]))
93
94        return palindromes
95
96    return {
97        'longest': longest_palindromic_substring,
98        'count': count_palindromic_substrings,
99        'is_palindrome': is_palindrome_range,
100        'all_palindromes': all_palindromic_substrings
101    }

Template 3: Z-Algorithm

1def z_algorithm(s):
2    """Z-algorithm: compute Z array where Z[i] = length of longest substring
3    starting from s[i] which is also a prefix of s"""
4
5    n = len(s)
6    z = [0] * n
7    left = right = 0
8
9    for i in range(1, n):
10        if i <= right:
11            # Use previously computed values
12            z[i] = min(right - i + 1, z[i - left])
13
14        # Try to extend match
15        while i + z[i] < n and s[z[i]] == s[i + z[i]]:
16            z[i] += 1
17
18        # Update window if we extended past right boundary
19        if i + z[i] - 1 > right:
20            left, right = i, i + z[i] - 1
21
22    return z
23
24def z_algorithm_applications():
25    """Applications of Z-algorithm"""
26
27    def pattern_search_z(text, pattern):
28        """Pattern searching using Z-algorithm"""
29        combined = pattern + "$" + text
30        z = z_algorithm(combined)
31        pattern_len = len(pattern)
32        matches = []
33
34        for i in range(pattern_len + 1, len(combined)):
35            if z[i] == pattern_len:
36                matches.append(i - pattern_len - 1)
37
38        return matches
39
40    def find_all_occurrences(s, pattern):
41        """Find all occurrences of pattern in string"""
42        return pattern_search_z(s, pattern)
43
44    def longest_prefix_suffix(s):
45        """Find longest prefix which is also suffix"""
46        z = z_algorithm(s)
47        n = len(s)
48
49        for i in range(n - 1, 0, -1):
50            if z[i] == n - i:
51                return s[:n - i]
52
53        return ""
54
55    def period_detection(s):
56        """Find the period of string using Z-algorithm"""
57        z = z_algorithm(s)
58        n = len(s)
59
60        for period in range(1, n):
61            if period + z[period] == n:
62                return period
63
64        return n  # String has no period shorter than itself
65
66    return {
67        'search': pattern_search_z,
68        'find_all': find_all_occurrences,
69        'prefix_suffix': longest_prefix_suffix,
70        'period': period_detection
71    }

Template 4: Advanced Rolling Hash

1class RollingHash:
2    """Advanced rolling hash for string problems"""
3
4    def __init__(self, s, base=256, mod=10**9 + 7):
5        self.s = s
6        self.n = len(s)
7        self.base = base
8        self.mod = mod
9
10        # Precompute hash values and powers
11        self.hash_values = [0] * (self.n + 1)
12        self.base_powers = [1] * (self.n + 1)
13
14        for i in range(self.n):
15            self.hash_values[i + 1] = (self.hash_values[i] * base + ord(s[i])) % mod
16            self.base_powers[i + 1] = (self.base_powers[i] * base) % mod
17
18    def get_hash(self, left, right):
19        """Get hash of substring s[left:right+1]"""
20        length = right - left + 1
21        result = (self.hash_values[right + 1] -
22                 self.hash_values[left] * self.base_powers[length]) % self.mod
23        return result if result >= 0 else result + self.mod
24
25    def compare_substrings(self, l1, r1, l2, r2):
26        """Compare two substrings using hash values"""
27        return (r1 - l1 == r2 - l2 and
28                self.get_hash(l1, r1) == self.get_hash(l2, r2))
29
30    def longest_duplicate_substring(self):
31        """Find longest duplicate substring (LC 1044)"""
32        def has_duplicate_of_length(length):
33            seen_hashes = set()
34            for i in range(self.n - length + 1):
35                substring_hash = self.get_hash(i, i + length - 1)
36                if substring_hash in seen_hashes:
37                    return i
38                seen_hashes.add(substring_hash)
39            return -1
40
41        # Binary search on length
42        left, right = 0, self.n - 1
43        result_start = -1
44
45        while left <= right:
46            mid = (left + right) // 2
47            start_pos = has_duplicate_of_length(mid)
48
49            if start_pos != -1:
50                result_start = start_pos
51                left = mid + 1
52            else:
53                right = mid - 1
54
55        return self.s[result_start:result_start + right] if result_start != -1 else ""
56
57    def distinct_echo_substrings(self):
58        """Count distinct echo substrings (LC 1316)"""
59        seen = set()
60        count = 0
61
62        for i in range(self.n):
63            for j in range(i + 1, self.n, 2):  # Only even lengths
64                mid = (i + j) // 2
65                if self.compare_substrings(i, mid, mid + 1, j):
66                    substring_hash = self.get_hash(i, j)
67                    if substring_hash not in seen:
68                        seen.add(substring_hash)
69                        count += 1
70
71        return count

Template 5: Suffix Array Construction

1def suffix_array_construction(s):
2    """Construct suffix array using counting sort (O(n log n))"""
3
4    def counting_sort(arr, key_func, max_val):
5        """Stable counting sort"""
6        count = [0] * (max_val + 1)
7        for item in arr:
8            count[key_func(item)] += 1
9
10        for i in range(1, len(count)):
11            count[i] += count[i - 1]
12
13        result = [0] * len(arr)
14        for i in range(len(arr) - 1, -1, -1):
15            key = key_func(arr[i])
16            count[key] -= 1
17            result[count[key]] = arr[i]
18
19        return result
20
21    n = len(s)
22    if n == 0:
23        return []
24
25    # Initial ranking based on first character
26    suffixes = list(range(n))
27    rank = [ord(c) for c in s]
28
29    k = 1
30    while k < n:
31        # Sort by (rank[i], rank[i + k])
32        def sort_key(i):
33            return (rank[i], rank[i + k] if i + k < n else -1)
34
35        # Find maximum rank for counting sort
36        max_rank = max(rank) + 1
37
38        # Sort by second key first, then by first key
39        suffixes = counting_sort(suffixes, lambda i: rank[i + k] if i + k < n else -1, max_rank)
40        suffixes = counting_sort(suffixes, lambda i: rank[i], max_rank)
41
42        # Update ranks
43        new_rank = [0] * n
44        for i in range(1, n):
45            if (rank[suffixes[i]], rank[suffixes[i] + k] if suffixes[i] + k < n else -1) == \
46               (rank[suffixes[i - 1]], rank[suffixes[i - 1] + k] if suffixes[i - 1] + k < n else -1):
47                new_rank[suffixes[i]] = new_rank[suffixes[i - 1]]
48            else:
49                new_rank[suffixes[i]] = i
50
51        rank = new_rank
52        k *= 2
53
54    return suffixes
55
56def lcp_array(s, suffix_array):
57    """Compute LCP (Longest Common Prefix) array"""
58    n = len(s)
59    if n == 0:
60        return []
61
62    # Inverse of suffix array
63    rank = [0] * n
64    for i in range(n):
65        rank[suffix_array[i]] = i
66
67    lcp = [0] * (n - 1)
68    h = 0
69
70    for i in range(n):
71        if rank[i] > 0:
72            j = suffix_array[rank[i] - 1]
73            while i + h < n and j + h < n and s[i + h] == s[j + h]:
74                h += 1
75            lcp[rank[i] - 1] = h
76            if h > 0:
77                h -= 1
78
79    return lcp

Problems by Pattern

Pattern Matching Problems

Problem LC # Best Algorithm Time Complexity Difficulty
Find Index of First Occurrence 28 KMP O(n + m) Medium
Repeated Substring Pattern 459 KMP/Z-Algorithm O(n) Easy
Shortest Palindrome 214 KMP + Reverse O(n) Hard

Palindrome Problems

Problem LC # Best Algorithm Time Complexity Difficulty
Longest Palindromic Substring 5 Manacher’s O(n) Medium
Palindromic Substrings 647 Manacher’s O(n) Medium
Shortest Palindrome 214 Manacher’s/KMP O(n) Hard

Advanced String Problems

Problem LC # Best Algorithm Time Complexity Difficulty
Longest Duplicate Substring 1044 Rolling Hash + Binary Search O(n log n) Hard
Distinct Echo Substrings 1316 Rolling Hash O(n²) Hard
Find All Anagrams 438 Rolling Hash O(n) Medium

LC Examples

2-1) Find Index of First Occurrence (LC 28) — KMP

Build LPS (failure) array for pattern; skip redundant comparisons on mismatch.

1// LC 28 - Find the Index of the First Occurrence in a String
2// IDEA: KMP — build LPS array; on mismatch jump to lps[j-1] instead of restarting
3// time = O(N+M), space = O(M)
4public int strStr(String haystack, String needle) {
5    int n = haystack.length(), m = needle.length();
6    int[] lps = new int[m];
7    for (int i = 1, len = 0; i < m; ) {
8        if (needle.charAt(i) == needle.charAt(len)) lps[i++] = ++len;
9        else if (len > 0) len = lps[len - 1];
10        else lps[i++] = 0;
11    }
12    for (int i = 0, j = 0; i < n; ) {
13        if (haystack.charAt(i) == needle.charAt(j)) { i++; j++; }
14        if (j == m) return i - j;
15        else if (i < n && haystack.charAt(i) != needle.charAt(j))
16            j = j > 0 ? lps[j - 1] : 0;
17        if (j == 0 && i < n && haystack.charAt(i) != needle.charAt(j)) i++;
18    }
19    return -1;
20}

1def strStr(haystack, needle):
2    """KMP implementation for string search"""
3    if not needle:
4        return 0
5
6    def compute_lps(pattern):
7        """Compute Longest Prefix Suffix array"""
8        m = len(pattern)
9        lps = [0] * m
10        length = 0
11        i = 1
12
13        while i < m:
14            if pattern[i] == pattern[length]:
15                length += 1
16                lps[i] = length
17                i += 1
18            else:
19                if length != 0:
20                    length = lps[length - 1]
21                else:
22                    lps[i] = 0
23                    i += 1
24
25        return lps
26
27    n, m = len(haystack), len(needle)
28    lps = compute_lps(needle)
29
30    i = j = 0
31    while i < n:
32        if haystack[i] == needle[j]:
33            i += 1
34            j += 1
35
36        if j == m:
37            return i - j
38        elif i < n and haystack[i] != needle[j]:
39            if j != 0:
40                j = lps[j - 1]
41            else:
42                i += 1
43
44    return -1

2-2) Longest Palindromic Substring (LC 5) — Expand Around Center

Try every center (odd and even length); expand while characters match.

1// LC 5 - Longest Palindromic Substring
2// IDEA: Expand around center for each of 2N-1 centers; track longest
3// time = O(N^2), space = O(1)
4public String longestPalindrome(String s) {
5    int start = 0, maxLen = 1;
6    for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
7        for (int d = 0; d <= 1; d++) {  // d=0: odd length, d=1: even length
8            int l = i, r = i + d;
9            while (l >= 0 && r < s.length() && s.charAt(l) == s.charAt(r)) { l--; r++; }
10            if (r - l - 1 > maxLen) { maxLen = r - l - 1; start = l + 1; }
11        }
12    }
13    return s.substring(start, start + maxLen);
14}

1def longestPalindrome(s):
2    """Manacher's algorithm for longest palindromic substring"""
3    if not s:
4        return ""
5
6    # Preprocess string
7    processed = "^#" + "#".join(s) + "#$"
8    n = len(processed)
9    p = [0] * n
10    center = right = 0
11
12    for i in range(1, n - 1):
13        mirror = 2 * center - i
14
15        if i < right:
16            p[i] = min(right - i, p[mirror])
17
18        # Try to expand
19        while processed[i + p[i] + 1] == processed[i - p[i] - 1]:
20            p[i] += 1
21
22        # Update center and right boundary
23        if i + p[i] > right:
24            center, right = i, i + p[i]
25
26    # Find longest palindrome
27    max_len = 0
28    center_index = 0
29    for i in range(1, n - 1):
30        if p[i] > max_len:
31            max_len = p[i]
32            center_index = i
33
34    start = (center_index - max_len) // 2
35    return s[start:start + max_len]

2-3) Longest Duplicate Substring (LC 1044) — Binary Search + Rolling Hash

Binary search on length; use Rabin-Karp rolling hash to check if duplicate of that length exists.

1// LC 1044 - Longest Duplicate Substring
2// IDEA: Binary search on length L; Rabin-Karp rolling hash checks duplicate in O(N)
3// time = O(N log N), space = O(N)
4public String longestDupSubstring(String s) {
5    int lo = 1, hi = s.length() - 1;
6    String ans = "";
7    while (lo <= hi) {
8        int mid = (lo + hi) / 2;
9        String dup = findDuplicate(s, mid);
10        if (dup != null) { ans = dup; lo = mid + 1; }
11        else hi = mid - 1;
12    }
13    return ans;
14}
15private String findDuplicate(String s, int len) {
16    long MOD = (1L << 61) - 1, BASE = 31;
17    long power = 1;
18    for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) power = power * BASE % MOD;
19    long hash = 0;
20    for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) hash = (hash * BASE + s.charAt(i)) % MOD;
21    Map<Long, List<Integer>> seen = new HashMap<>();
22    seen.computeIfAbsent(hash, k -> new ArrayList<>()).add(0);
23    for (int i = len; i < s.length(); i++) {
24        hash = (hash * BASE - s.charAt(i - len) * power % MOD + s.charAt(i) + MOD) % MOD;
25        int start = i - len + 1;
26        if (seen.containsKey(hash)) {
27            String sub = s.substring(start, start + len);
28            for (int prev : seen.get(hash))
29                if (s.substring(prev, prev + len).equals(sub)) return sub;
30        }
31        seen.computeIfAbsent(hash, k -> new ArrayList<>()).add(start);
32    }
33    return null;
34}

1def longestDupSubstring(s):
2    """Rolling hash with binary search"""
3
4    def has_duplicate(length):
5        base = 256
6        mod = 2**63 - 1
7        base_power = pow(base, length, mod)
8
9        current_hash = 0
10        for i in range(length):
11            current_hash = (current_hash * base + ord(s[i])) % mod
12
13        seen = {current_hash}
14
15        for i in range(length, len(s)):
16            # Remove leftmost character and add rightmost
17            current_hash = (current_hash - ord(s[i - length]) * base_power) % mod
18            current_hash = (current_hash * base + ord(s[i])) % mod
19
20            if current_hash in seen:
21                return i - length + 1
22            seen.add(current_hash)
23
24        return -1
25
26    left, right = 0, len(s) - 1
27    result_start = 0
28
29    while left <= right:
30        mid = (left + right) // 2
31        start_pos = has_duplicate(mid)
32
33        if start_pos != -1:
34            result_start = start_pos
35            left = mid + 1
36        else:
37            right = mid - 1
38
39    return s[result_start:result_start + right] if right > 0 else ""

Advanced Techniques

Multiple Pattern Matching

1class AhoCorasick:
2    """Aho-Corasick algorithm for multiple pattern matching"""
3
4    def __init__(self):
5        self.trie = {}
6        self.failure = {}
7        self.output = {}
8
9    def add_pattern(self, pattern, pattern_id):
10        """Add pattern to trie"""
11        node = self.trie
12        for char in pattern:
13            if char not in node:
14                node[char] = {}
15            node = node[char]
16        node['$'] = pattern_id
17
18    def build_failure_function(self):
19        """Build failure function for Aho-Corasick"""
20        from collections import deque
21
22        queue = deque()
23        self.failure[id(self.trie)] = self.trie
24
25        # Initialize first level
26        for char, child in self.trie.items():
27            if char != '$':
28                self.failure[id(child)] = self.trie
29                queue.append((child, char))
30
31        # Build failure links using BFS
32        while queue:
33            current, char = queue.popleft()
34            current_id = id(current)
35
36            for next_char, next_node in current.items():
37                if next_char == '$':
38                    continue
39
40                queue.append((next_node, next_char))
41
42                # Find failure link
43                failure_node = self.failure[current_id]
44                while failure_node != self.trie and next_char not in failure_node:
45                    failure_node = self.failure[id(failure_node)]
46
47                if next_char in failure_node:
48                    self.failure[id(next_node)] = failure_node[next_char]
49                else:
50                    self.failure[id(next_node)] = self.trie
51
52    def search_all(self, text):
53        """Find all pattern occurrences in text"""
54        current = self.trie
55        results = []
56
57        for i, char in enumerate(text):
58            # Follow failure links
59            while current != self.trie and char not in current:
60                current = self.failure[id(current)]
61
62            if char in current:
63                current = current[char]
64
65                # Check for matches
66                temp = current
67                while temp != self.trie:
68                    if '$' in temp:
69                        pattern_id = temp['$']
70                        results.append((i, pattern_id))
71                    temp = self.failure[id(temp)]
72
73        return results

String Hashing Optimizations

1class DoubleHash:
2    """Double hashing to reduce collision probability"""
3
4    def __init__(self, s):
5        self.s = s
6        self.n = len(s)
7
8        # Two different bases and moduli
9        self.base1, self.mod1 = 257, 10**9 + 7
10        self.base2, self.mod2 = 263, 10**9 + 9
11
12        self.hash1 = [0] * (self.n + 1)
13        self.hash2 = [0] * (self.n + 1)
14        self.pow1 = [1] * (self.n + 1)
15        self.pow2 = [1] * (self.n + 1)
16
17        for i in range(self.n):
18            self.hash1[i + 1] = (self.hash1[i] * self.base1 + ord(s[i])) % self.mod1
19            self.hash2[i + 1] = (self.hash2[i] * self.base2 + ord(s[i])) % self.mod2
20            self.pow1[i + 1] = (self.pow1[i] * self.base1) % self.mod1
21            self.pow2[i + 1] = (self.pow2[i] * self.base2) % self.mod2
22
23    def get_hash(self, left, right):
24        """Get double hash of substring"""
25        length = right - left + 1
26        h1 = (self.hash1[right + 1] - self.hash1[left] * self.pow1[length]) % self.mod1
27        h2 = (self.hash2[right + 1] - self.hash2[left] * self.pow2[length]) % self.mod2
28        return (h1, h2)

Performance Optimization Tips

Algorithm Selection Guide

1def choose_string_algorithm(problem_characteristics):
2    """Guide for choosing optimal string algorithm"""
3
4    if problem_characteristics['type'] == 'pattern_matching':
5        if problem_characteristics['single_pattern']:
6            return "KMP or Z-Algorithm"
7        else:
8            return "Aho-Corasick"
9
10    elif problem_characteristics['type'] == 'palindromes':
11        if problem_characteristics['all_palindromes']:
12            return "Manacher's Algorithm"
13        else:
14            return "Expand around centers"
15
16    elif problem_characteristics['type'] == 'substring_queries':
17        if problem_characteristics['many_queries']:
18            return "Rolling Hash or Suffix Array"
19        else:
20            return "Simple comparison"
21
22    elif problem_characteristics['type'] == 'string_matching':
23        if problem_characteristics['approximate']:
24            return "Edit distance DP"
25        else:
26            return "KMP or Rolling Hash"

Summary & Quick Reference

Algorithm Complexity Comparison

Algorithm Time Complexity Space Complexity Best Use Case
KMP O(n + m) O(m) Single pattern search
Manacher’s O(n) O(n) All palindromes
Z-Algorithm O(n) O(n) Pattern matching variants
Rolling Hash O(n) O(1) amortized Multiple queries
Suffix Array O(n log n) O(n) Complex string operations

Common Mistakes & Tips

🚫 Common Mistakes:

  • Not handling empty strings or patterns
  • Off-by-one errors in index calculations
  • Hash collision issues with single hash
  • Incorrect failure function computation

✅ Best Practices:

  • Always validate input strings
  • Use double hashing for collision resistance
  • Preprocess strings when multiple queries expected
  • Choose algorithm based on problem constraints
  • Test with edge cases and long strings

Interview Tips

  1. Identify the core requirement: Pattern matching, palindromes, or queries
  2. Choose optimal algorithm: Based on time/space constraints
  3. Handle edge cases: Empty strings, single characters
  4. Consider preprocessing: For multiple queries
  5. Implement carefully: Index management is critical
  6. Test thoroughly: With various string lengths and patterns

This comprehensive advanced string algorithms cheatsheet covers the most sophisticated techniques for optimal string processing and pattern matching.

GitHub • Documentation • Report Issues
